untranslatable_reason: Signal requires ranking stocks by relative past one-year returns and selecting top/bottom quantiles. This requires cross-sectional ranking across a universe of stocks, which is not expressible using single-stock technical indicators. The 12-month lookback period (returns indicator) can be approximated, but the relative ranking and portfolio construction logic (long high-momentum, short low-momentum) requires portfolio-level operations not supported by the single-instrument indicator set.
translation_notes: UMD (Unique Momentum Direction) is a cross-sectional momentum factor requiring comparative analysis across multiple securities. The allowed indicators are single-instrument technical indicators. A partial approximation uses returns over the lookback period, but loses the critical 'relative' ranking component that defines the strategy. Proper implementation requires a factor construction engine, not point-in-time technical conditions.
